openingMessages.text: Bus leaves in 20. Don't be the reason we're late.
lol remember last year's hotel wifi
clip of Riley missing the easy pass lol

values.label: Courage
values.blurb: We say the hard thing, even when it's easier to stay quiet.

values.label: Excellence
values.blurb: We hold the standard when no one's watching.

values.label: Respect
values.blurb: Everyone gets treated like they belong here.

values.label: Trust
values.blurb: We have each other's backs, on and off the floor.

values.label: Care
values.blurb: We look out for each other, especially when someone's struggling.

values.label: Accountability
values.blurb: We own our mistakes instead of hiding them.

scenarios.title: The first-year in the corner
scenarios.description: A few starters are riffing on Riley — the easy pass from warmups, the way they talk, the gear they showed up in. Riley is half-laughing, looking at the floor. The room is waiting to see what happens next.

options.label: Laugh along
options.note: You laughed with them. It kept things easy — and Riley got quieter for the rest of the trip.

options.label: Stay silent
options.note: You said nothing. It blew over — but silence reads as agreement when you're in the room.

options.label: Check in with Riley privately later
options.note: Later, you found Riley alone and let them know you saw it. They opened up more than you expected.

options.label: Redirect the conversation
options.note: You pulled the room somewhere else without making it a confrontation. The moment passed, the dignity stayed.

options.label: Speak up in the moment
options.note: You said it plainly: that's enough. The room went still — then reset. Riley caught your eye.

scenarios.title: After the rivalry loss
scenarios.description: The buzzer just went. Someone mutters that Casey's turnover cost the game, loud enough for everyone to hear. Heads turn. Casey stares at the floor.

options.label: Agree it was on Casey
options.note: The blame stuck to one person. Easy story — and Casey stopped taking risks after that.

options.label: Let it slide
options.note: Nobody stopped it. The room learned that when things go wrong, someone gets fed to the wolves.

options.label: Name your own part in the loss
options.note: You owned a mistake of your own first. It shifted the room from blame to 'what do we fix.'

options.label: Shut the blame down
options.note: You said it straight: we win and lose as a team. Casey exhaled. So did a few others.

scenarios.title: The shortcut
scenarios.description: Two starters are heading out instead of doing the recovery session the staff laid out. 'It's optional, basically.' A couple of younger players are watching to see if it really is.

options.label: Head out with them
options.note: You skipped it too. The standard quietly dropped a notch — and the rookies noticed who set it.

options.label: Do the session, say nothing
options.note: You stayed and did the work without comment. Quiet example — some followed, some didn't.

options.label: Pull everyone back in
options.note: You made it a team thing instead of a chore. More people stayed than you'd have guessed.
